Title: Alterations in hippocampal serotonergic and INSR function in streptozotocin induced diabetic rats exposed to stress: neuroprotective role of pyridoxine and Aegle marmelose

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulation of stress-related behaviours and anxiety; serotonergic signaling homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Diabetes downregulates hippocampal 5-HTT expression, associated with reduced serotonergic function and increased anxiety-like behavior; pyridoxine, insulin, and Aegle marmelose treatments restore 5-HTT expression toward control levels
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time PCR; immunohistochemistry/confocal imaging; metabolite profiling; elevated plus maze behavioral analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hippocampal 5-HTT mRNA was significantly downregulated in streptozotocin-induced diabetic rats. 2. 5-HTT immunostaining was also reduced in diabetic hippocampus and was restored by treatment. 3. Recovery of 5-HTT expression correlated with normalization of hippocampal serotonin status and anxiety-related behavior.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in hippocampus; significantly reduced under streptozotocin-induced diabetes and restored by pyridoxine, insulin, and Aegle marmelose treatments
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Streptozotocin-induced diabetes; insulin treatment; pyridoxine treatment; Aegle marmelose leaf extract treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to 5-HTT as a key mediator of serotonin recycling and homeostatic serotonergic signaling in hippocampus; no direct nutrient-biosynthetic catalytic role was tested
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR using TaqMan assay; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hippocampus/presynaptic serotonergic terminals per study context

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy metabolism and glucose homeostasis; supports cognitive and neuronal functions in brain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Blood; brain hippocampus studied for receptor expression rather than metabolite accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Streptozotocin-induced diabetes downregulates hippocampal INSR mRNA, and treatment with insulin, pyridoxine, and Aegle marmelose reverses expression toward control, consistent with improved glucose homeostasis and neuroprotection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Real-time PCR; blood glucose measurement; pharmacological treatment comparison
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hippocampal INSR mRNA was significantly reduced in diabetic rats. 2. Insulin, pyridoxine, and Aegle marmelose treatments restored INSR expression toward control levels. 3. Recovery of INSR expression was associated with reduced hyperglycemia and improved neurobehavioral outcomes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in hippocampus; downregulated in diabetic rats and restored by treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Streptozotocin-induced diabetes; insulin treatment; pyridoxine treatment; Aegle marmelose leaf extract treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: INSR is central to diabetic phenotype interpretation in this paper but is not a biosynthetic pathway enzyme or transcription factor
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exogenous insulin treatment may cause hypoglycemia, as discussed by authors
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR using TaqMan assay; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hippocampus in rat brain per study context

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neurotransmission affecting anxiety, stress responses, hippocampal plasticity and cognition
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Diabetes reduces hippocampal 5-HT2A receptor binding and HTR2A mRNA/protein expression; pyridoxine with insulin and/or Aegle marmelose restores receptor abundance and serotonergic function
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Radioligand receptor binding with [3H]ketanserin; Real-time PCR; immunohistochemistry/confocal imaging; behavioral assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Streptozotocin-induced diabetes significantly decreased 5-HT2A receptor Bmax and altered Kd in hippocampus. 2. HTR2A mRNA and immunostaining were significantly reduced in diabetic rats. 3. Pyridoxine alone and in combination therapies restored receptor expression/binding and improved anxiety-related behavior.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in hippocampus; significantly downregulated in diabetic rats and restored by treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Streptozotocin-induced diabetes; insulin treatment; pyridoxine treatment; Aegle marmelose leaf extract treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a classic nutrient-metabolism gene, HTR2A is a core experimental target mediating serotonergic regulation linked to diabetic stress phenotype
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR using TaqMan assay; receptor binding assays; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hippocampus in rat brain per study context
